Hey there, this is Dave D. from GKIC and I have with me a very special guest on this super important bonus CD, how to create a kick butt newsletter that will help you generate a ton of referrals. I have with me the best guy on the planet to teach you about this. He is the newsletter pro and he is our go to expert here at GKIC for all things that have to do with newsletters. At Super Conference, Sean won GKIC's highly covered marketer of the year award. His company, the newsletter pro has grown by just leaks and bounds having gone from just one full time employee to over 30 full time employees in just the last few years and he is out paced to mail over 3.5 million newsletters for his clients in 2014. Now what is important about that to you is that there is no way he would have had this explosive growth if his newsletters weren't working for his clients. So today we are going to talk about how you can properly put together a client newsletter, customer newsletter, patient newsletter so you can generate more referrals and then we are going to talk about some other benefits of the newsletter as well. So Sean, thank you for joining us on this very special bonus CD. Thanks for having me Dave, I appreciate it. So Sean, let's talk about the first thing is I don't think that people even realize that they need a customer newsletter or client newsletter or patient newsletter. And so I don't have to keep saying that I'm just going to say client newsletter from now on. Just so it doesn't matter if it's a customer, client, a patient, a guest for a restaurant, it doesn't make any difference. So let's just start off with the most basic question, why is having a newsletter important and what are some of the benefits that you get from sending out a newsletter? And by the way, first of all, we are talking about a print newsletter here. So how about all of that? So I just asked you about, I don't know, 30 questions in about a minute. But you are a young guy so you can memorize all the stuff and answer all the questions. Yeah, yeah, I'll do my best. So yeah, so I mean there's a couple of different things of why you should have it as part of your marketing strategy. And in fact, actually it goes back to a really good example. I'll give you a really good example here in a second of what I'm about to explain. But Dan always says people are ready to buy when they're ready to buy and not when you're ready to sell them. They're going to do business with you whenever they're ready. And so from a prospect standpoint, one great reason is that you actually get a way to communicate with the prospects and continue to engage with them, let them know who you are, be able to remember your name. From your client standpoint, it allows you to provide additional value, sell additional services to them that they may not realize that you have. At GKC, I hear all the time that you can't get bored with your own stories or your own products or your own services. And you may think you've explained to a client before about this ABC thing that you've got over here, but they don't even remember that you do it. Or that that's a service you offer and they're going somewhere else and getting it. So it allows you to engage them. It allows you to build relationships with them so you can retain more clients. And then via relationships also get referrals. So today, in this day and age, people are a little more stingy with their referrals. I think they've been burnt over the years. And so they prefer to refer people that they know and like and trust and they know are going to do a good job for them. So that relationship aspect is super important. But let me tell you a story and it's interesting. This just happened actually yesterday. So something that happened in my business, which was where we got a guy who was referred into us. And he was referred into us a number of months ago. And he didn't sign up, which happens. Although referrals typically, those are the ones that almost always sign up right away, but this guy didn't. And so he gets referred in and he gets on our newsletter, he starts receiving our newsletter. He then runs into us at a super conference. And so we talked to him a little bit more again. Someone else recommends that he uses our service who is at super conference. You know, we were all kind of talking at the same time. He doesn't buy. We start sending him, we keep sending him our newsletters. He gets our weekly email, right? So he's seeing us. He then sees us at InfusionCon where we were exhibiting there and does not sign up at InfusionCon. But takes a step and goes on to my website and requests some more information. Gets on a phone call with me yesterday and yesterday finally finds up. So I added it up because we do some, we have pretty good tracking. So I was able to see kind of all the times we saw him. And then I knew I'd just recently seen him at two events. And I had to kind of get in front of this guy roughly nine times, do the nine or ten times to, before he decided to buy. Even though he was referred in from another vendor who he trusts, who he's been using for years.
